meta.coalitionStrikesSource: Curated OSINT seed + ISW/CTP ArcGIS FeatureServer
meta.iswSource: ISW/CTP — Interactive Map: U.S. and Israeli Strikes in Iran (2026)

timeline.event: Hamas Attack on Israel
timeline.description: Hamas launched a coordinated surprise offensive on Israel with 3000+ rockets and ~2500 militants breaching the Gaza-Israel barrier. Iran-backed groups celebrated the attacks.
timeline.impactOutcome: Sparked the broader Middle East crisis; Iran's Axis of Resistance activated

timeline.event: Iran-backed Militia Attacks Begin
timeline.description: Iran-backed proxy forces ramped up strikes on US and Israeli targets in Iraq and Syria; Hezbollah opened a northern Lebanon front; over 200 attacks in weeks.
timeline.impactOutcome: 170+ attacks on US military assets in Iraq, Syria, and Jordan between Oct 2023 – Feb 2024

timeline.event: US Airstrikes on Iran-backed Facilities
timeline.description: US ordered airstrikes on two Iran-backed facilities in retaliation for proxy attacks on US personnel and assets.
timeline.impactOutcome: US signals willingness to hit Iranian proxies directly

timeline.event: Houthi Red Sea Campaign Begins
timeline.description: Houthis began targeting international shipping lanes and Israeli-linked vessels in the Red Sea in solidarity with Gaza, escalating global trade disruption.
timeline.impactOutcome: Major shipping disruption; triggered US-led naval coalition Operation Prosperity Guardian

timeline.event: Jordan Tower 22 Attack
timeline.description: Iran-backed militias struck a US military outpost in Jordan (Tower 22), killing 3 US service members and injuring dozens.
timeline.impactOutcome: First US fatalities from proxy attacks; major escalation point

timeline.event: Assad Regime Falls in Syria
timeline.description: The Bashar al-Assad regime collapsed, depriving Iran of a critical land corridor and strategic ally in its Axis of Resistance.
timeline.impactOutcome: Iran's regional influence severely weakened; proxy network fragmented

timeline.event: US Retaliatory Strikes – Operation Inherent Resolve
timeline.description: US launched airstrikes on 85 Iran-affiliated targets across Iraq and Syria in response to the Tower 22 attack.
timeline.impactOutcome: Largest US retaliation against Iran-backed groups since 2003

timeline.event: Israel Strikes Iranian Consulate in Damascus
timeline.description: Israel bombed the Iranian consulate complex in Damascus, killing multiple senior Iranian Revolutionary Guard officers.
timeline.impactOutcome: Iran vowed retaliation; major direct escalation

timeline.event: Iran Launches First Direct Strike on Israel
timeline.description: Iran and its allies launched 300+ drones and ballistic missiles directly at Israel. Israeli defenses (with US support) intercepted most projectiles.
timeline.impactOutcome: First ever direct Iranian strike on Israel; US directly helped Israel defend

timeline.event: Israel Retaliatory Strike on Iran
timeline.description: Israel launched limited retaliatory strikes targeting Iranian air defenses. Analysts described it as signaling a desire to de-escalate.
timeline.impactOutcome: Tensions cooled temporarily; conflict remained at proxy level for months

timeline.event: Iran Fires 180 Ballistic Missiles at Israel
timeline.description: Following Israeli assassinations of Hamas and Hezbollah leaders, Iran launched 180 ballistic missiles against Israel.
timeline.impactOutcome: Major escalation; Israel vowed and carried out strikes on Iranian air defenses and missile production facilities

timeline.event: Israel Strikes Iranian Air Defenses & Missile Sites
timeline.description: Israel conducted its largest-ever direct attack on Iran, targeting air defense systems and missile production facilities.
timeline.impactOutcome: Iran's air defenses significantly degraded; Hezbollah and Hamas leadership decimated

timeline.event: Trump Returns – Maximum Pressure Resumed
timeline.description: President Trump restored maximum pressure economic sanctions campaign against Iran upon returning to office. Also initiated first direct US-Iran nuclear talks since 2018.
timeline.impactOutcome: Dual track of pressure + diplomacy; Iran's economy further strained

timeline.event: Iran Protests Escalate
timeline.description: Largest protests in Iran since 1979 erupted over a weakened economy and crumbling infrastructure; Iranian security forces responded with extreme violence.
timeline.impactOutcome: Regime's domestic legitimacy severely undermined

timeline.event: Twelve-Day War Begins – Israel Strikes Iran
timeline.description: Israel launched a surprise attack targeting key Iranian military and nuclear facilities; included targeted assassinations of Iranian military leaders and nuclear scientists.
timeline.impactOutcome: Iran's defenses and nuclear program significantly damaged

timeline.event: Iran and Houthis Strike Israel Jointly
timeline.description: Iran and the Houthis jointly struck Jaffa with long-range ballistic missiles — the first such joint Iran-militia strike directly inside Israel.
timeline.impactOutcome: Marked major escalation; US joined Israeli air operations

timeline.event: US Airstrikes on Iranian Nuclear Sites
timeline.description: The US conducted airstrikes aimed at destroying Iran's nuclear facilities during the Twelve-Day War, effectively joining Israel's military campaign.
timeline.impactOutcome: Iran's nuclear program severely disrupted; 12-day war ended in ceasefire

timeline.event: Iran Massacres Civilian Protesters
timeline.description: Iranian security forces massacred thousands of civilians in cracking down on mass protests, drawing global condemnation.
timeline.impactOutcome: Trump threatened military action; US began largest regional military buildup since 2003 Iraq invasion

timeline.event: Trump Calls for Regime Change in Iran
timeline.description: Trump publicly stated regime change in Iran would be 'the best thing that could happen'. A day later US officials confirmed preparations for sustained military operations.
timeline.impactOutcome: Set stage for military action; Iran alerted to imminent threat

timeline.event: Trump State of the Union – Iran Nuclear Warning
timeline.description: Trump accused Iran of reviving nuclear weapons development and advancing missile capabilities threatening Europe and US bases. Warned the US was prepared to act.
timeline.impactOutcome: Diplomatic ultimatum effectively issued to Iran

timeline.event: Iran Claims Historic Nuclear Deal 'Within Reach'
timeline.description: Iranian FM Araghchi stated a 'historic' agreement to avert military conflict was within reach ahead of renewed talks in Geneva. Iran reiterated opposition to nuclear weapons.
timeline.impactOutcome: Diplomatic window opened; ultimately failed to prevent strikes

timeline.event: Operation Epic Fury – US & Israel Strike Iran
timeline.description: US and Israel launched nearly 900 strikes in 12 hours targeting Iranian missiles, air defenses, military infrastructure, and leadership. Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was assassinated. Strikes occurred during active negotiations.
timeline.impactOutcome: Iran's supreme leader killed; largest US-Israel joint operation in history

timeline.event: Iran Retaliates – Missile and Drone Strikes
timeline.location: Israel / US military bases / Arab Gulf States
timeline.description: Iran responded to US-Israeli strikes with massive missile and drone barrages against Israel, US bases across the Middle East, and US-allied Gulf Arab states.
timeline.impactOutcome: Widespread regional destabilization; GCC states targeted

timeline.event: Mojtaba Khamenei Appointed New Supreme Leader
timeline.description: Following Ali Khamenei's assassination, Mojtaba Khamenei (his son) was selected as the new Supreme Leader of Iran.
timeline.impactOutcome: Iran's political continuity maintained; regime did not collapse

timeline.event: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz
timeline.description: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, through which ~20% of global oil supply passes, disrupting global trade and energy markets.
timeline.impactOutcome: Global oil prices surged; US threatened 'extensive attacks' on Iranian energy infrastructure if not reopened by April 6

timeline.event: Iran Protests Regime – Internal Pressure Mounts
timeline.description: Widespread protests erupted inside Iran, motivated by economic collapse and weakened infrastructure following the strikes. Security forces used extensive force to suppress protests.
timeline.impactOutcome: Regime faced deepest domestic legitimacy crisis in its history

timeline.event: Trump Offers Talks – Sanctions-for-Enrichment Deal
timeline.description: Trump stated openness to talks: Iran ending all nuclear enrichment in exchange for full US sanctions relief. Iran denied formal talks while reportedly having conditions.
timeline.impactOutcome: Conflict ongoing; ceasefire negotiations uncertain as of April 2026

timeline.event: US Ultimatum – Reopen Strait of Hormuz
timeline.description: Trump threatened extensive attacks on Iranian energy sites if Iran did not reopen the Strait of Hormuz by April 6 2026. More US troops ordered into region.
timeline.impactOutcome: Strait status unresolved as of early April 2026; situation remains volatile

timeline.event: Pakistan-Brokered Ceasefire Takes Effect
timeline.description: Pakistan secured an initial ceasefire halting the heaviest US-Israeli and Iranian exchanges. Mediators opened a channel for direct talks.
timeline.impactOutcome: Reduced intensity after March peak; set stage for Islamabad process

timeline.event: First US–Iran Direct Talks Since War Began
timeline.description: Highest-level US-Iran engagement since diplomatic ties severed in 1979 hosted with Pakistani facilitation.
timeline.impactOutcome: First formal diplomacy since Operation Epic Fury

timeline.event: Post-Ceasefire Friction Over Strait of Hormuz
timeline.description: Tit-for-tat incidents over commercial shipping and blockade enforcement persisted despite truce efforts. Iran targeted vessels it said strayed from approved routes; US Central Command struck Iranian military sites.
timeline.impactOutcome: Oil markets remained volatile; trust in ceasefire fragile

timeline.event: US and Iran Agree Final MOU Text
timeline.description: Washington and Tehran reached agreed final language for a framework to end the war brokered primarily by Pakistan with Qatar Saudi Arabia Turkey and Egypt.
timeline.impactOutcome: Opened path to formal Islamabad Memorandum signing

timeline.event: Islamabad Memorandum Signed — 60-Day Roadmap
timeline.keyActors: Donald Trump / Masoud Pezeshkian / Pakistan mediators
timeline.description: 14-point MOU declares immediate end to military operations on all fronts including Lebanon; mandates Hormuz reopening 60-day window for final deal and nuclear/sanctions negotiations.
timeline.impactOutcome: Largest diplomatic milestone since war began; ceasefire extended institutionally

timeline.event: Burgenstock Summit — De-confliction & Working Groups
timeline.description: Switzerland hosted implementation talks establishing a Lebanon de-confliction cell and working groups on nuclear issues sanctions and dispute resolution.
timeline.impactOutcome: Created institutional follow-through beyond initial MOU

timeline.event: Post-MOU Military Incidents — Hormuz and Gulf Bases
timeline.description: Iran struck US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain; US retaliated against Iranian missile and drone storage sites after Hormuz shipping disputes. Both sides accused the other of violating the MOU.
timeline.impactOutcome: Tested durability of ceasefire; Gulf states condemned Iranian strikes

timeline.event: Doha Technical Talks — MOU Implementation
timeline.description: Qatari and Pakistani mediators held technical sessions on Hormuz navigation nuclear issues and a US-Iran hotline to report MOU violations. Talks paused until after Khamenei funeral.
timeline.impactOutcome: Diplomacy continuing but no comprehensive final deal yet

timeline.event: Khamenei Funeral Processions Begin
timeline.description: Multi-day funeral ceremonies for former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei (killed Feb 28) began in Tehran. Iran vowed to seek justice against US and Israel.
timeline.impactOutcome: Domestic consolidation under Mojtaba Khamenei; diplomacy on hold during mourning period

usStrikeNames: US Retaliatory Strikes – Operation Inherent Resolve
US Airstrikes on Iranian Nuclear Sites

osintWaves.description: Forty-first wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 12 (March 11), afternoon. After a nine-hour pause, IRGC launched a fresh ballistic missile salvo from Iran targeting central and northern Israel at approximately 1:45 PM local time. Al Jazeera reported heavy, multi-warhead projectiles with interceptions observed over Israel. At least one missile reportedly reached the Tel Aviv area. Sirens sounded in central and northern Israel.
osintWaves.payload: Ballistic missiles with heavy, multi-warhead projectiles targeting central and northern Israel
osintWaves.targets: Central Israel and northern Israel. Sirens in central and northern Israel. At least one missile reportedly reached Tel Aviv area.
osintWaves.interceptionReport: Interceptions observed over Israel. At least one missile reportedly reached the Tel Aviv area. Heavy, multi-warhead projectiles reported by Al Jazeera.
osintWaves.damage: At least one missile reportedly reached Tel Aviv area. Full damage assessment pending.

osintWaves.codenameEnglish: First Wave Under the New Ayatollah
osintWaves.description: Thirty-fifth wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 11 (March 10), overnight. IRGC launched ballistic missile with cluster warhead at central Israel. Submunitions scattered across at least six sites in Yehud, Or Yehuda, Holon, Bat Yam, and Rishon Letzion. One man killed at construction site in Yehud, two men seriously wounded at same site. Woman moderately wounded by debris in Rishon Letzion. Iranian state broadcaster IRIB hailed this as the 'first wave of missiles under Mojtaba Khamenei towards occupied territories.'
osintWaves.payload: Ballistic missile with cluster warhead; submunitions dispersed across 6+ sites
osintWaves.targets: Yehud (construction site — primary impact, 1 killed, 2 seriously wounded); Or Yehuda; Holon; Bat Yam; Rishon Letzion (1 moderately wounded)
osintWaves.interceptionReport: Cluster warhead submunitions not intercepted; multiple impact sites across central Israel. No specific interception details reported for this salvo.
osintWaves.damage: Construction site in Yehud struck. Submunitions impacted across six sites in Yehud, Or Yehuda, Holon, Bat Yam, and Rishon Letzion.

osintWaves.description: Thirty-sixth wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 11 (March 10). IRGC launched 17 ballistic missiles and 6 drones at Qatar. Qatar Armed Forces successfully intercepted all incoming projectiles with no casualties or damage reported. Part of ongoing Iranian targeting of Gulf States hosting US military assets. Qatari Ministry of Defence released video of interceptions.
osintWaves.targets: Qatar — likely targeting Al Udeid Air Base and other US military facilities
osintWaves.interceptionReport: Qatar Armed Forces successfully intercepted all 17 ballistic missiles and 6 drones. No casualties or damage reported. Qatar MoD released video of interceptions.
osintWaves.damage: No damage reported. All projectiles intercepted.

osintWaves.description: Thirty-third wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 10 (March 9), late evening. Codename "At Your Service, Khamenei." Heaviest wave of the day. Large numbers of solid-fuel Khaibar Shekan missiles with one-ton warheads launched at Tel Aviv — 10+ impacts reported. IRGC Aerospace Force drones reportedly destroyed Sdot Micha satellite communications center south of Tel Aviv. US Navy 5th Fleet base also targeted with precision-guided missiles. IRGC Ruhollah Corps intercepted a cruise missile over central Iran and destroyed an Orbiter-4 drone over Isfahan.
osintWaves.payload: Large numbers of solid-fuel Khaibar Shekan missiles with one-ton warheads; IRGC Aerospace Force drones
osintWaves.targets: Tel Aviv (10+ missile impacts reported); US Navy 5th Fleet base; Sdot Micha satellite communications center (reportedly destroyed by drones)
osintWaves.interceptionReport: IRGC Ruhollah Corps intercepted and destroyed a cruise missile over central Iran. An Orbiter-4 reconnaissance drone belonging to 'Zionist-American enemy' was destroyed over Isfahan.
osintWaves.damage: 10+ Khaibar Shekan missiles reportedly struck Tel Aviv, penetrating Iron Dome. Sdot Micha satellite communications center south of Tel Aviv reportedly destroyed by IRGC drones.

osintWaves.description: Thirty-fourth wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 10 (March 9), evening. Hezbollah long-range missile barrage targeting central Israel. 16 lightly wounded in Ramle including from kindergarten hit. Hezbollah claimed targets were IDF Home Front Command HQ (Rehavam Base) in Ramle and satellite communications station in Haela Valley near Beit Shemesh.
osintWaves.targets: Ramle (kindergarten and infrastructure hit); Mateh Yehuda Regional Council (open area); claimed target: IDF Home Front Command HQ (Rehavam Base) in Ramle; claimed target: satellite communications station in Haela Valley near Beit Shemesh
osintWaves.interceptionReport: Air defenses intercepted some missiles; others hit open areas and Ramle. No warning sirens sounded in Ramle. IDF destroyed three Hezbollah launchers within one hour via airstrikes.
osintWaves.damage: Kindergarten/daycare facility in Ramle struck and damaged. Infrastructure damage in Ramle. No warning sirens sounded.

osintWaves.description: Thirty-second wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 10 (March 9), evening. Codename "At Your Service, Khamenei" (also rendered as "The noose will tighten"). IRGC Aerospace Force launched Qadr and Khorramshahr ballistic missiles at sites in northern and central Israel. IRGC stated "heavy fire will continue to rain down" and warned of relentless pressure.
osintWaves.payload: Qadr and Khorramshahr ballistic missiles targeting northern and central Israel
osintWaves.targets: Sites in northern and central Israel

osintWaves.description: Thirty-first wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 10 (March 9), afternoon. Codename "Labbaik Ya Khamenei" — dedicated to new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. Joint IRGC Aerospace Force and IRGC Navy operation. Featured super-heavy missiles (Qadr, Khorramshahr, Kheibar Shekan) plus naval loitering drones and cruise missiles. Targeted five strategic US bases including 5th Fleet HQ, Israeli military sites in Tel Aviv and Haifa, and US Al-Adiri helicopter base. Cluster munition strike in Bat Yam dispersed 24 submunitions across 6 locations. 2 killed in central Israel. Bahrain: 32 injured including children in strike on Sitra island.
osintWaves.launchSiteDesc: Iran — IRGC Aerospace Force and IRGC Navy
osintWaves.payload: Super-heavy missiles: Qadr, Khorramshahr, Kheibar Shekan ballistic missiles; IRGC Navy loitering drones and cruise missiles
osintWaves.targets: Five strategic US bases (incl. US Navy 5th Fleet HQ); Israeli military sites; Cluster warhead struck Yehud, Or Yehuda, Holon, Bat Yam, Rishon LeZion
osintWaves.interceptionReport: NATO downed a second Iranian missile over Turkey on March 9. UAE detected 15 ballistic missiles and 18 UAVs from Iran (12 destroyed, 3 fell in sea).
osintWaves.damage: Cluster munition warhead struck at least 6 sites across central Israel — Yehud, Or Yehuda, Holon, Bat Yam, Rishon LeZion. Construction site hit in Yehud (1 killed, 1 critically wounded). Man seriously wounded in Or Yehuda. Woman moderately injured in Rishon LeZion from submunition partial detonation.

osintWaves.description: Thirtieth wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 10 (March 9), morning. First IRGC action since Mojtaba Khamenei became Supreme Leader on March 8. Featured Khorramshahr, Fattah hypersonic, and Kheibar Shekan ballistic missiles plus strategic drones. Sirens sounded in Netanya and Western Galilee. Iranian TV claimed complete success.
osintWaves.payload: Khorramshahr, Fattah (hypersonic), Kheibar Shekan ballistic missiles + strategic drones
osintWaves.targets: US and Israeli positions; sirens in Netanya, Western Galilee, northern Israel
osintWaves.damage: Iranian TV claimed "complete success"; specific damage details sparse.

osintWaves.description: Twenty-ninth wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 9 (March 8), afternoon. Codename 'Ya Ali Ibn Abi Talib'. IRGC Aerospace Force launched new generation of missiles hitting targets in Tel Aviv, the Naqab (Negev), and US airbase in region. Details still emerging.
osintWaves.targets: Tel Aviv; Naqab (Negev); US airbase in region (unspecified)

osintWaves.description: Twenty-eighth wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 9 (March 8). Featured Qadr, Emad (700kg-1 ton warheads), and Kheibar Shekan missiles (1,450 km range, terminal maneuverability, high strike accuracy). Targeted Tel Aviv, Be'er Sheva, and Al-Azraq Air Base (Jordan). Iranian media claimed THAAD radar systems destroyed in preceding 24h period.
osintWaves.payload: Qadr, Emad (700kg-1 ton warheads), Kheibar Shekan ballistic missiles (1,450 km range, terminal maneuverability)
osintWaves.targets: Tel Aviv; Be'er Sheva (Bir al-Sabe'); Al-Azraq Air Base (Jordan)
osintWaves.interceptionReport: Reports of large explosions in Tel Aviv, Jerusalem, and west of Ramallah. Air defenses active.
osintWaves.damage: Iranian media claimed direct ballistic missile impact in central Israel; large explosions reported in Tel Aviv. Unconfirmed independently.

osintWaves.description: Twenty-seventh wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 8 (March 7), evening. Codename 'Ya Fatimah al-Zahra'. Khorramshahr-4 heavy ballistic and Fattah-2 hypersonic missiles with cluster warheads plus suicide drones. Targeted Haifa oil refinery and US positions at Camp Udairi/Buehring (Kuwait). Iranian media claimed fires at Haifa refinery.
osintWaves.payload: Khorramshahr-4 heavy ballistic missiles, Fattah-2 hypersonic missiles, cluster warheads, suicide drones
osintWaves.targets: Haifa oil refinery; US positions in Kuwait (Camp Udairi / Camp Buehring). Cluster warheads confirmed over Israel.
osintWaves.interceptionReport: Heavy sirens in Haifa Bay. Kuwaiti and US defenses on high alert. Explosions heard near border.
osintWaves.damage: Iranian state media claimed drone swarm hit Haifa refinery causing large fires. Unconfirmed.

osintWaves.description: Twenty-sixth wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 8 (March 7). Combined drone-missile operation under codename 'Ya Heydar Karrar'. New-generation Emad, Ghadr, and multi-warhead Kheibar missiles with cluster warheads struck targets across northern and southern Israel including Jerusalem (cluster missile intercepted over city per Anadolu Agency and Israel National News). Sdot Micha strategic military facility targeted. Concurrent Iran army drone strikes against US bases at Al-Minhad (UAE) and Camp Udairi (Kuwait). IRGC claimed destruction of enemy radar systems in previous waves made striking targets easier.
osintWaves.payload: New-generation Emad, Ghadr, and multi-warhead Kheibar missiles; drones
osintWaves.targets: Targets from north to south of Israel hit with precision, including Jerusalem (cluster missile intercepted over city per Anadolu Agency/Israel National News). Sdot Micha strategic military facility (radar). Also Iran army drone strikes against US bases: Al-Minhad Air Base (UAE) and Camp Udairi (Kuwait).
osintWaves.interceptionReport: Israeli military reported intercepting over 110 drones since start of day. Acknowledged impacts in open areas and near military sites. Sirens in Kiryat Shmona and Upper Galilee.
osintWaves.damage: Damage to radar installations at Sdot Micha. Sirens in Kiryat Shmona and Upper Galilee.

osintWaves.description: Twenty-fifth wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 8 (March 7). Codename 'Al-Muqaddis / Ali ibn Abi Talib'. Fattah hypersonic and Emad missiles with Shahed-238 suicide drone swarms. Targeted joint US-Israeli military centers, Al-Dhafra and Al-Minhad Air Bases (UAE).
osintWaves.payload: Fateh, Imad (Emad), and strategic missiles; drones
osintWaves.targets: Joint US-Israeli military centers and logistical support. Al-Dhafra Air Base (UAE), Al-Minhad Air Base (UAE). IRGC claimed precision strikes on radar and satellite comms centers.
osintWaves.interceptionReport: US and UAE defenses engaged. IRGC claimed precision strikes on radar and satellite communication centers.

osintWaves.description: Twenty-fourth wave of Operation True Promise 4. Day 8 (March 7), dawn. Codename 'Ya Mahdi'. Kheibar Shekan, Emad, Ghadr ballistic missiles with cluster warheads targeting Tel Aviv. At least 3 impacts confirmed. IRGC released launch footage.
osintWaves.payload: Kheibar Shekan, Emad, and Ghadr ballistic missiles
osintWaves.targets: Tel Aviv ("heart of the occupied territories"). At least 3 missile impacts confirmed in Tel Aviv per Al Jazeera. IRGC claimed strikes on strategic facilities.
osintWaves.interceptionReport: Israeli Home Front Command reported sirens across Tel Aviv metro. Al Jazeera reported at least 3 missiles bypassed defenses.
osintWaves.damage: Minor structural damage in Tel Aviv and Givatayim. Three missile impacts confirmed in Tel Aviv metro.

osintWaves.description: Twenty-third wave of Operation True Promise 4. Announced late evening March 6 (Day 7). IRGC stated this phase would involve 'some of the world's most advanced missiles.' Targets reported across Israel and Gulf states including Kuwait, Qatar, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, and UAE. Israeli military confirmed intercepting missiles. Details remain sparse as the wave was announced only hours ago; this is a preliminary entry pending further OSINT confirmation.
osintWaves.payload: New-generation solid- and liquid-fuel missiles (Khaibar-Shekan, Rezvan variants); drones
osintWaves.targets: US bases: Sheikh Isa (Bahrain), Juffair (Bahrain), Ali al-Salem (Kuwait), al-Azraq (Jordan). Israel: Be'er Sheva (advanced technology centers, cybersecurity facilities, military support centers).
osintWaves.interceptionReport: Israeli military confirmed defence systems intercepting missiles launched from Iran.

osintWaves.codenameEnglish: Ya Hossein ibn Ali (peace be upon him)
osintWaves.description: Twenty-second wave of Operation True Promise 4. Codename 'Ya Hossein ibn Ali.' Significant escalation in weapon sophistication: first wave to combine Khorramshahr-4 (~1,500 kg warhead, Mach 16), Kheibar Shekan, and Fattah hypersonic missiles simultaneously. Targeted Tel Aviv, Ben Gurion Airport, Haifa military centers, plus Al Udeid and Al Dhafra air bases in the Gulf. IRGC framed as retaliation for Minab school strike. Iranian state media stated most missiles used in the war to date were from 2012-2014 production, and new generation weapons had only been deployed in rare cases until this wave.
osintWaves.payload: Khorramshahr-4 ballistic missiles (~1,500 kg warhead, Mach 16 exo-atmospheric / Mach 8 atmospheric), Kheibar Shekan ballistic missiles, Fattah hypersonic missiles, drones
osintWaves.targets: Tel Aviv, Ben Gurion Airport, Israeli Air Force military centers in Haifa, US and Israeli positions across the Persian Gulf including Al Udeid Air Base (Qatar) and Al Dhafra Air Base (UAE). Retaliation for Minab school strike.
osintWaves.interceptionReport: Israeli military confirmed defence systems intercepting missiles launched from Iran. No injuries reported in Israel.
osintWaves.damage: IRGC claims strikes on Tel Aviv, Ben Gurion Airport, military centers in Haifa. Claims Al Udeid radar systems, air traffic control, satellite stations, and fuel depots obliterated. Claims Al Dhafra early warning radar and critical infrastructure destroyed by precision strikes and drone swarms. No confirmed Israeli casualties.

osintWaves.description: Twenty-first wave of Operation True Promise 4. Codename 'Ya Mu'izz al-Mu'minin.' IRGC Aerospace Force launched combined suicide drone swarms and Kheibar Shekan ballistic missiles with cluster warheads at Tel Aviv early Friday morning on Day 7 of the conflict. IRGC stated the tactic was designed to overwhelm Israel's multi-layered air defences. Iran vowed 'more intense' operations in coming days. Cumulative munitions prior to this wave reportedly exceeded 2,000 drones and 600 missiles.
osintWaves.payload: Kheibar Shekan ballistic missiles with cluster munition warheads, suicide drone swarms
osintWaves.targets: Tel Aviv; combined drone swarms and Kheibar cluster missiles designed to saturate and overwhelm multi-layered air defences
osintWaves.interceptionReport: IRGC claims Kheibar cluster warheads fragmented mid-flight, releasing dozens of submunitions that rendered Iron Dome, David's Sling, and Arrow largely ineffective. Independent verification unavailable.
osintWaves.damage: IRGC claims cluster warhead submunitions dispersed across target areas. No specific damage reports confirmed by Israeli sources.

osintWaves.codenameEnglish: Ya Hassan ibn Ali (peace be upon him)
osintWaves.description: Nineteenth wave; codename 'Ya Hassan ibn Ali.' Combined missile and drone operation using Khorramshahr-4 ballistic missiles with 1-ton warheads. IRGC claims bypassed THAAD and struck Israeli Defense Ministry and Ben Gurion Airport. Also targeted USS Abraham Lincoln and Amazon data center in Bahrain. IDF reports most missiles intercepted; no casualties.
osintWaves.payload: Khorramshahr-4 ballistic missiles (1-ton warheads), explosive drones
osintWaves.targets: Israeli Defense Ministry complex Tel Aviv, Ben Gurion Airport, Jerusalem; US military positions in Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar; USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72); Amazon data center Bahrain
osintWaves.interceptionReport: IDF reports all overnight ballistic missiles intercepted; one morning missile struck open area in central town
osintWaves.damage: IRGC claims strikes on Israeli Defense Ministry Tel Aviv, Ben Gurion Airport, 7+ US/Israeli radar systems destroyed. One missile struck open area in central Israel. Amazon facility Bahrain allegedly suffered extensive damage.

osintWaves.description: Seventeenth wave; codename 'Sacred: O Messenger of Allah.' 40+ ballistic missiles fired by IRGC Aerospace Force. Religious-nationalist messaging increasingly prominent.
osintWaves.targets: American and Israeli targets; Israeli Ministry of Defense building (Tel Aviv); Ben Gurion Airport; 7+ advanced US radars destroyed

osintWaves.description: Sixteenth wave; critical shift - IRGC Ground Forces entered the battlefield with three simultaneous operations and 230 attack drones. Multi-domain offensive. Aerospace Division also struck 'heart and northern parts of occupied territories.'
osintWaves.launchSiteDesc: Western Iran, IRGC ground force positions
osintWaves.targets: HaKirya (IDF general staff and Ministry of War) Tel Aviv, strategic infrastructure Bnei Brak, military targets Beit Hakfa; Kuwait, Iraq, Saudi Arabia, UAE (hundreds of drones); Greek F-16s intercept Shahed drones near Cyprus; Turkish air defences intercept missiles over Hatay
osintWaves.damage: IRGC claims strikes on HaKirya (IDF general staff and Ministry of War), strategic infrastructure in Bnei Brak, military targets in Beit Hakfa

osintWaves.description: Fifteenth wave; continued geographic expansion. Also targeted 10 key points at Sheikh Isa Air Base Bahrain. Air raid sirens in central Israel. IRGC claims to have shot down 7 Israeli drones.
osintWaves.targets: Al-Udeid Air Base Qatar, Israeli positions; US Consulate Al Seef Dubai (drones, fire); Fujairah Oil Industry Zone UAE; Hamad International Airport Qatar (intercepted); AWS facilities UAE and Bahrain
osintWaves.damage: Strikes on Al-Udeid Air Base Qatar and Israeli positions. IRGC claims 7 Israeli drones shot down (Hermes, Heron, MQ-9). Suspected cluster/fragmentation warhead detonation in Bnei Brak/Petah Tikva causing blast damage; 7 wounded (1 moderate, 6 light) per Haaretz.

osintWaves.description: Fourteenth wave; IRGC Navy large-scale attack on US air base in Bahrain with drones and missiles.
osintWaves.targets: US air base Sheikh Isa area Bahrain; US embassy Riyadh Saudi Arabia (2 drones, fire); CIA headquarters Riyadh (drone hit); Saudi Arabia downed 9 drones
osintWaves.damage: IRGC claims main command building and HQ of US air base destroyed; fuel tanks set on fire. 20 drones and 3 missiles claimed to have hit targets.

osintWaves.description: Thirteenth wave; IRGC Navy drone unit targeting Kuwaiti-based US Marine installations. Marked first clear indication of different IRGC branches coordinating successive waves under unified command.
osintWaves.targets: US Navy Marine base at Arifjan base Kuwait, Ali Al-Salim air base Kuwait
osintWaves.damage: IRGC claims strikes on US Marine Corps installations at Arifjan base Kuwait

osintWaves.description: Twelfth wave; IRGC Navy-led maritime campaign. 26 drones and 5 ballistic missiles targeting US assets in three Gulf countries plus Strait of Hormuz maritime target.
osintWaves.payload: 26 attack drones, 5 ballistic missiles, Fattah-2 hypersonic missile
osintWaves.targets: US Arifjan base Kuwait, US Al Minhad command center UAE, US naval facilities Bahrain, fuel tanker Athens Nova in Strait of Hormuz; Port Fujairah UAE; Dubai UAE (ballistic missile); Sulaymaniyah Iraqi Kurdistan; Erbil Iraq
osintWaves.damage: IRGC Navy claims: Arifjan base Kuwait hit by 12 drones in two phases; Al Minhad base UAE hit by 6 drones and 5 ballistic missiles; US naval facilities Bahrain destroyed by 6 drones; fuel tanker Athens Nova in Strait of Hormuz struck by 2 drones and still burning.

osintWaves.description: Eleventh wave; naval and aerospace IRGC units. Targeted communications and intelligence infrastructure. IRGC claims 500 sites targeted in preceding 48 hours.
osintWaves.targets: US intelligence centers and military support warehouses in Persian Gulf, Israeli army communications complex Beersheba, 20+ sites in Tel Aviv/West Jerusalem/Galilee; Paphos Airport Cyprus (evacuated); Akrotiri Cyprus (sirens); Aramco Ras Tanura Saudi Arabia (refinery fire); Qatar LNG facilities; Mina Salman port Bahrain; Duqm/Oman maritime targets; US-Jordanian embassy evacuated
osintWaves.damage: IRGC claims strikes on US intelligence centers, military support warehouses in Persian Gulf, Israeli army communications complex in Beersheba, 20+ points in Tel Aviv/West Jerusalem/Galilee

osintWaves.description: Tenth wave; featured advanced Kheibar Shekan maneuvering missiles (MaRV-equipped, Mach 8, 500-1500kg warhead). IRGC stated it 'opened the great gates of fire upon the occupied territories.'
osintWaves.targets: Israeli government complex Tel Aviv, military/security centers Haifa, East Jerusalem; Netanyahu's office (Tel Aviv); Saudi Aramco facility
osintWaves.damage: IRGC claims hits on Israeli government complex in Tel Aviv, military/security centers in Haifa, and East Jerusalem. Strike in Beersheba injured at least 10.
osintWaves.idfStatement: IDF assessing damage from advanced missile strikes

osintWaves.description: Ninth wave; IRGC claims 700+ drones and hundreds of missiles launched since operation began. Cluster munition warhead used against Tel Aviv area (12 injuries).
osintWaves.targets: US naval assets, Israeli defense systems, drones across multiple regions; RAF Akrotiri Cyprus (1 drone hit airfield, 5 intercepted, minor damage)
osintWaves.damage: IRGC claims strikes on 60 strategic targets and 500 combined US/Israeli sites since operation start. Cluster munition warhead strike near Tel Aviv injured 12.

osintWaves.description: Eighth wave; continuation of wave 7 operations. IRGC claims massive US casualties (560 killed/wounded - unverified). Large-scale drone deployments.
osintWaves.targets: Continued strikes on American and Israeli targets, regional US bases
osintWaves.damage: IRGC claims 560 US military killed/wounded across waves 7-8 (unverified). Continued tanker fires.

osintWaves.description: Seventh wave; massive expansion to maritime targets. IRGC claims 4 ballistic missiles fired at USS Abraham Lincoln carrier. Three oil tankers struck. Major Gulf base attacks.
osintWaves.targets: US/British oil tankers in Persian Gulf/Strait of Hormuz, Ali Al-Salem naval base Kuwait, Mohammed Al-Ahmad Naval Base Kuwait, Mina Salman US naval base Bahrain, USS Abraham Lincoln; Prince Sultan Airbase and King Khalid International Airport Saudi Arabia (intercepted); AWS data center UAE
osintWaves.damage: Three US/British oil tankers in Persian Gulf struck and reportedly engulfed in fire. Ali Al-Salem naval base Kuwait shut down. Heavy damage to US base in Bahrain.

osintWaves.description: Sixth wave; escalation to targeting Israeli political-military command infrastructure in dense urban Tel Aviv. IRGC signaling ability to strike leadership centers.
osintWaves.targets: Tel Nof Air Base, HaKirya headquarters (Israeli Defense Ministry/military command) Tel Aviv, Israeli Navy base Haifa, Haifa military naval yard
osintWaves.damage: IRGC claims hits on Tel Nof Air Base, HaKirya HQ, and Haifa naval installations

osintWaves.description: Fifth wave; shift to maritime and Gulf targets. IRGC Navy involvement. MSP and MST logistics vessels in Indian Ocean reportedly struck (unverified).
osintWaves.targets: American vessels in Indian Ocean, Abdullah Mubarak naval base Kuwait; Two missiles fired towards British bases on Cyprus (denied by Cyprus MoD)
osintWaves.damage: IRGC claims strikes on American vessels and Abdullah Mubarak naval base in Kuwait. Oil tanker reportedly struck in Indian Ocean.

osintWaves.description: Fourth wave; continued expansion of targeting to military-industrial sites in Israel and Gulf bases.
osintWaves.targets: Beit Shams military-industrial complex, Ishtod military-industrial complex, US bases in Kuwait and Iraq
osintWaves.damage: Continued strikes on Gulf and Israeli targets

osintWaves.description: Third wave; IRGC announced using more advanced missiles than True Promise 3. Expanded targeting to Gulf logistics infrastructure.
osintWaves.payload: Advanced ballistic missiles, drones, cruise missiles
osintWaves.targets: Israeli naval base in Haifa, warship dock in Haifa, Ramat David Air Base, HaKirya (Ministry of War) Tel Aviv, Abdullah Al-Mubarak Air Base Kuwait, Harir base Iraq, Prince Sultan Air Base Saudi Arabia; Crowne Plaza Hotel Manama Bahrain; Etihad Towers Abu Dhabi (drone intercepted, debris damage); French base Camp de la Paix Abu Dhabi; Al-Salam Naval Base Abu Dhabi; Saudi oil refinery
osintWaves.damage: Abdullah Al-Mubarak Air Base in Kuwait reportedly disabled. Damage to Harir base in Iraq.

osintWaves.description: Second wave continuing initial retaliatory barrage on Feb 28; 2-4 missiles per salvo pattern observed.
osintWaves.targets: Tel Aviv, US bases in Iraq

osintWaves.description: First wave of Operation True Promise 4; large-scale coordinated attack with drones to saturate defenses followed by ballistic missiles. Launched hours after US-Israeli Operation Epic Fury/Roaring Lion strikes on Iran.
osintWaves.launchSiteDesc: Multiple sites in western Iran including Kermanshah and Tabriz regions
osintWaves.payload: Emad and Ghadr ballistic missiles, Shahed-136 drones
osintWaves.targets: Nevatim airbase, Tel Aviv, US military facilities in Persian Gulf; USN Fifth Fleet HQ Bahrain; Port Jebel Ali UAE; Duqm port Oman (2 drones, 1 injured); Tankers off Oman coast
osintWaves.interceptionReport: Multi-layered coalition defense. US Navy destroyers engaged with SM-3. Arrow-3 exoatmospheric intercepts. Allied jets intercepted drones over Iraq/Jordan.
osintWaves.damage: Impacts reported in Tel Aviv (1 fatality, 21 injuries) and Bnei Brak (several injuries). Damage to US facilities in Gulf region.
osintWaves.idfStatement: IDF reports high interception rate in coordination with allies; minor damage to one base

osintWaves.targets: Military and logistical centers — final wave minutes before US-brokered ceasefire
osintWaves.damage: Beersheba residential building struck at 5:40am IST. 4 killed, 20 injured. Final missiles until 7:10am. IRGC called it a 'historic lesson'.

osintWaves.targets: Military/strategic targets — new tactics for precision and destructiveness

osintWaves.payload: 40 missiles (solid + liquid fuel), Kheibar Shekan multi-warhead variant (first use)
osintWaves.targets: Ben Gurion Airport, biological research centers, C2 facilities
osintWaves.interceptionReport: 10 of 27 ballistic missiles hit Israel — highest single-wave penetration rate (~37%)
osintWaves.damage: Ben Gurion Airport temporarily shut down; flight diversions for 36 hours

osintWaves.payload: Combined missiles and drones — large combined operation
osintWaves.targets: Strategic targets — large combined operation

osintWaves.payload: Shahed-136 drones and precision missiles — new innovations in missile selection, target choice, and guidance paths
osintWaves.targets: 14 strategic military sites: Hadera power plant, Haifa oil refinery, Ovda airbase (cyber command), Kiryat Gat semiconductor zone, Rafael center Haifa
osintWaves.damage: 14 strategic military sites targeted including Hadera power plant, Haifa oil refinery, Ovda airbase (cyber command), Kiryat Gat semiconductor zone, Rafael center Haifa. Bazan Oil Refinery struck (3 killed, refinery shut down). Haifa Power Plant struck (fire).

osintWaves.payload: Fattah-1 (first combat use in TP3), Emad, Ghadr, others
osintWaves.targets: Air defense radars, military targets — Fattah used to destroy radar systems, clearing path for follow-on missiles
osintWaves.damage: Fattah-1 used to destroy air defense radar systems

osintWaves.targets: Israeli airbases from which jets attacked Iran
osintWaves.damage: Nevatim Airbase absorbed ~32 MRBMs; direct hit on hardened F-35I shelter (IDF claimed no aircraft damaged). Glilot intel base hit. Tel Nof base hit. Kirya HQ damaged.

osintWaves.targets: Haifa, Tel Aviv — continued bombardment
Tel Aviv, Haifa — enhanced intelligence-guided targeting
Strategic/sensitive targets in Tel Aviv, Haifa — C2 systems targeted
osintWaves.damage: Targeted command and control systems

osintWaves.targets: Israeli C2 centers, Haifa, Tel Aviv; Bat Yam struck
osintWaves.damage: Bat Yam residential building struck — 9 killed (incl. 5 Ukrainian nationals), 200+ injured, 61 buildings damaged. Worst single incident of the war.

osintWaves.targets: Haifa oil refinery, military targets
osintWaves.damage: Haifa oil refinery directly hit and shut down

osintWaves.targets: Tel Aviv, Haifa, other cities
osintWaves.damage: Continued escalation — strikes on urban areas

osintWaves.targets: Occupied territories — penetrated multiple defense layers
Haifa, Galilee
osintWaves.damage: State of emergency declared across Israel

osintWaves.targets: Military centers, air bases
osintWaves.damage: Serious damage to sensitive military centers in Tel Aviv area

osintWaves.description: Second wave included Iran's Fattah-1 hypersonic missile in its first confirmed combat use, alongside additional Emad and Kheibar Shekan missiles. This wave targeted the Mossad/Unit 8200 headquarters complex at Glilot near Tel Aviv in addition to the airbases. Satellite imagery later showed missiles landing approximately 500m from the Mossad complex.
osintWaves.launchSiteDesc: IRGC Aerospace Force launch sites in western Iran
osintWaves.payload: Fattah-1 hypersonic missiles, Emad, Kheibar Shekan ballistic missiles
osintWaves.targets: Mossad/Unit 8200 HQ at Glilot (north Tel Aviv), Nevatim Airbase, Tel Nof Airbase
osintWaves.interceptionReport: Most missiles intercepted. Closest impact to Mossad HQ was approximately 500m. Fattah-1 hypersonic missile performance debated — Israel claimed successful interception, Iran claimed successful penetration.
osintWaves.damage: Missiles landed near Mossad/Unit 8200 HQ (~500m); homes and restaurant damaged in north Tel Aviv area; additional impacts at Nevatim and Tel Nof

osintWaves.description: First wave of approximately 100 ballistic missiles targeting Israeli military airbases and intelligence facilities. Unlike TP1, no drones or cruise missiles were used — Iran opted for a purely ballistic approach to minimize warning time. Missiles included Emad, Ghadr-H, Ghadr-F, and Kheibar Shekan. Iran gave only a few hours' warning to Arab states (compared to 72 hours in TP1).
osintWaves.launchSiteDesc: IRGC Aerospace Force launch sites in western Iran
osintWaves.payload: Emad, Ghadr-H, Ghadr-F, Kheibar Shekan ballistic missiles
osintWaves.targets: Nevatim Airbase (primary), Tel Nof Airbase, Hatzerim Airbase
osintWaves.interceptionReport: Arrow-2 and Arrow-3 engaged incoming ballistic missiles. US Navy destroyers fired SM-3 interceptors. Despite interceptions, 20-32 missiles struck Nevatim Airbase area and 3 struck Tel Nof area per satellite analysis.
osintWaves.damage: Nevatim Airbase: hangar and taxiway damaged (20-32 impacts confirmed by satellite); Tel Nof Airbase: several impacts; Gedera: school building hit
osintWaves.idfStatement: IDF reported approximately 200 ballistic missiles fired in two waves

osintWaves.description: Second wave of 120 ballistic missiles (Emad, Ghadr, Kheibar Shekan, possibly Shahab-3B) timed to arrive after drone wave had engaged Israeli air defense systems. Ballistic missiles launched later but arrived within minutes due to much higher velocity. Several missiles struck Nevatim Airbase area causing minor damage.
osintWaves.launchSiteDesc: IRGC Aerospace Force launch sites in western and central Iran
osintWaves.payload: 120 ballistic missiles including Emad, Ghadr (Ghadr-S/H/F variants), Kheibar Shekan, possibly Shahab-3B
osintWaves.targets: Nevatim Airbase (primary), Ramon Airbase area, sites in Negev desert and Golan Heights
osintWaves.interceptionReport: Arrow-2 and Arrow-3 intercepted ballistic missiles, with Arrow-3 performing exoatmospheric interceptions. SM-3 from US Navy destroyers was used in combat for the first time. A small number of ballistic missiles struck Nevatim Airbase causing minor damage; the base remained fully operational.
osintWaves.damage: Minor damage to Nevatim Airbase (base remained operational); debris fell in Arad region
osintWaves.idfStatement: 99% of incoming weapons intercepted; minor damage to Nevatim Airbase

osintWaves.description: First wave of Shahed-136 loitering munitions and Paveh cruise missiles intended to saturate Israeli air defenses (Iron Dome, David's Sling) ahead of the ballistic missile wave. Drones launched several hours before ballistic missiles due to slower flight speed (~185 km/h for Shahed-136 vs minutes for ballistic missiles).
osintWaves.launchSiteDesc: Multiple sites across Iran; Shahed drones also launched from Iraq and Yemen by proxies
osintWaves.payload: 170 Shahed-136 loitering munitions and 30 Paveh cruise missiles
osintWaves.targets: Nevatim Airbase, sites in Negev desert and Israeli-occupied Golan Heights
osintWaves.interceptionReport: US CENTCOM reported destroying more than 80 one-way attack drones and at least 6 ballistic missiles. Jordan and UK RAF also intercepted drones over Jordanian airspace. France contributed to interception efforts.
osintWaves.damage: Minimal — most munitions intercepted before reaching Israeli airspace
osintWaves.idfStatement: IDF reported 170 drones, 30 cruise missiles, 120 ballistic missiles launched; 99% interception rate

osintWaves.description: Twentieth wave of Operation True Promise 4. Small number of ballistic missiles with cluster munition warheads and drones launched at central Israel. Eighth Iranian attack since midnight. Sirens sounded in Tel Aviv and surrounding area. Police confirmed cluster bomb warhead; missile fragments and submunitions impacted several areas. One impact in a central town sparked a fire. No immediate injuries reported. IDF Home Front Command cleared shelters after attack. Iran has fired at least six cluster missiles at Israel since the war began per Haaretz reporting.
osintWaves.payload: Kheibar Shekan ballistic missiles with cluster munition warheads (~20 submunitions per warhead, ~2.5 kg explosive each, ~8 km dispersal radius), drones
osintWaves.targets: Tel Aviv and surrounding area, central Israel. Sirens sounded across central Israel. Eighth Iranian attack since midnight.
osintWaves.damage: Missile fragments and submunitions from cluster bomb warhead impacted several areas in central Israel, causing damage. One impact site in a central town sparked a fire. Police confirmed cluster bomb warhead. Each submunition carries ~2.5 kg explosive, ~20 submunitions per warhead, dispersal radius ~8 km. Submunitions designed to explode on impact; unexploded submunitions pose landmine-like hazard.
osintWaves.idfStatement: IDF Home Front Command cleared civilians to leave shelters but advised remaining close. No immediate injuries reported by medics.

osintWaves.description: Eighteenth wave; codename references Imam Hassan Mojtaba in celebration of his holy birthday. Ghadr-class ballistic missiles confirmed launched by IRGC.

interceptAnalytics.methodologyNote: Official IDF/CENTCOM rates (often ~99%) reflect press statements, not per-sortie BDA. Contested estimates stratify by threat class: Shahed drones intercept better under saturation (~50–60% in TP1 CENTCOM math) than maneuvering ballistic (~5–8%) or Fattah hypersonic (~<10%). Where satellite impact counts exist (e.g. Nevatim 20–32 craters), those override headline claims. Video/OSINT suggests overall leak-through in the low teens — not high-90s.

coalitionSummary.dataGapNote: Iran attack waves are documented per-sortie in the OSINT repo (63 waves). Curated coalition seed has narrative campaign-level records; ISW/CTP ArcGIS adds ~1,180 geolocated US/Israel strike points (410 confirmed) for Feb–May 2026. DoD still does not publish per-sortie US ordnance manifests.

Shin posted a photo showing smoke plumes and fires at Bahman Port in Qeshm. Iranian media reported that combined force strikes targeted the Doha fishing pier in Qeshm (next to Bahman Port). The pier is located right next to a Basij base.

The deputy governor of Khuzestan told Iran's Mehr news agency that the US/Israeli coalition struck several points along Minu island.
The deputy governor of Khuzestan told Iran's Mehr news agency that the US/Israeli coalition struck two areas in Abadan

iswSummary.sourceNote: Proprietary geodata from the Institute for the Study of War (ISW) and Critical Threats Project (CTP), published via ArcGIS StoryMaps. Green = confirmed airstrike, blue = reported, yellow = explosion w/ footage. Use for cross-reference — not redistributed as raw geodata.

coalitionAircraft.role_in_operations: Primary long-range strike fighter and air superiority. Backbone of IAF deep-strike capability. Used for CAP and drone/cruise missile interception during True Promise operations.
key_features: Conformal fuel tanks for extended range without drag penalty
Israeli-specific avionics and EW suite
Optimized for long-range strike missions into Iran

coalitionAircraft.role_in_operations: Multirole strike and interception. Block 52-based with Israeli avionics, conformal fuel tanks, and extended range. Participated in drone interception during True Promise operations.
key_features: Israeli conformal fuel tanks extending range significantly
Dorsal spine housing Israeli-developed avionics and EW
Largest single fighter fleet in the IAF

coalitionAircraft.role_in_operations: Israel's premier stealth platform. Used for intelligence gathering, air superiority, and reportedly has overflown Iranian airspace. Israeli modifications include custom mission computers and EW suites.
key_features: Full low-observable stealth design
Israeli-specific mission computers and plug-and-play weapons integration
360-degree sensor fusion via AN/AAQ-37 DAS
First stealth fighter in Middle East

coalitionAircraft.role_in_operations: USN carrier-based strike and air defense. Launched from carriers in the Gulf/Eastern Med to intercept Iranian drones and cruise missiles during True Promise operations.
key_features: Carrier-based — operates from Nimitz/Ford-class CVNs
33% more internal fuel than legacy Hornet
AN/APG-79 AESA radar for BVR engagement
Primary USN fleet defense fighter

coalitionAircraft.role_in_operations: RAF deployed Typhoons from RAF Akrotiri (Cyprus) and bases in Qatar. Successfully shot down multiple Iranian Shahed drones during True Promise I (April 2024) and 2026 operations.
key_features: Supercruise capability (>Mach 1 without afterburner)
MBDA Meteor beyond-visual-range missile
Deployed from RAF Akrotiri and Qatar for Iranian drone interception
CAPTOR-E AESA radar (Tranche 3)

coalitionAircraft.role_in_operations: USAF deployed F-16s from regional bases (Al Udeid, Al Dhafra, others) for air defense, CAP, and SEAD missions in support of coalition interception during True Promise operations.
key_features: Most-produced Western fighter (4,600+ built)
Block 50/52 HARM Targeting System for SEAD
Widely deployed across CENTCOM bases

coalitionAircraft.fleet_notes: 43x F-16A + 18x F-16B; Block 70/72 with APG-83 AESA on order
coalitionAircraft.role_in_operations: Jordan scrambled F-16s to intercept Iranian drones transiting Jordanian airspace en route to Israel during True Promise I (April 2024). Intercepted a significant number of the 170+ drones, demonstrating Jordanian-Israeli defense cooperation.
key_features: Mid-Life Update (MLU) with modernized avionics
Block 70/72 (F-16V) order will give AESA radar capability
Key role in TP1 drone interception over Jordanian airspace

munitionsEstimates.methodologyNote: Guestimates only — not official expenditure data. Iran totals combine explicit OSINT munition counts (16 waves), parsed payload strings, and per-threat-class imputation for waves without numbers. Coalition totals anchor on ISW geolocated strike points (1–3 munitions per point) and narrative strike counts (e.g. Epic Fury ~900). Per-system figures allocate those totals by weapon mentions on OSINT/coalition records. Kaggle 'missile attacks' are daily event counts, not rounds fired — shown as a cross-check.

currentStatus.headline: Fragile ceasefire under Islamabad Memorandum
currentStatus.summary: The Feb–Mar 2026 hot war has shifted to a negotiated pause. The Islamabad Memorandum (signed June 17–18) established a 60-day roadmap, Hormuz reopening commitments, and Lebanon de-confliction — but tit-for-tat incidents and Doha technical talks show implementation remains contested.
watchItems: US–Iran hotline for MOU violation reporting (established post-Doha)
Khamenei funeral processions (Jul 4–9) — diplomacy on hold
Iran warnings of proportionate response if MOU breached
Israel–Hezbollah fighting in southern Lebanon despite MOU Lebanon clause
Post-MOU strikes: US bases in Kuwait/Bahrain; US retaliatory Gulf strikes
